headline: What's driving the meteoric rise of the DRAM ETF
summary: The Roundhill Memory ETF (DRAM) has become the fastest-growing ETF ever, soaring past $6.5 billion in managed assets. This comes as memory chip stocks like Micron Technology (MU) surge by 180% year-to-date in 2026 and Sandisk (SNDK) skyrocketing over 3,200% over the past year alone. Roundhill Investments CEO Dave Mazza — whose firm manages the fund — comes on Market Catalyst to talk about the ETF's growth and the supply and demand dynamics at play with AI data center projects.

headline: The Semiconductor Upcycle Is Being Driven by Memory Pricing, Not Unit Growth
summary: Introduction For decades, semiconductor cycles followed a fairly predictable sequence. Demand improved, companies bought more equipment to make more chips, unit shipments increased so that supply exceed demand, and pricing eventually weakened as oversupply returned to the market. Investors therefore learned to focus primarily on unit growth because pricing historically lagged demand rather than led ... The Semiconductor Upcycle Is Being Driven by Memory Pricing, Not Unit Growth

The Semiconductor Upcycle Is Being Driven by Memory Pricing, Not Unit Growth
